notable.notable_blurb: 4th Governor of Connecticut Colony; Hartford founder

narrative_blocks.header: Twice Governor of Connecticut Colony in the 1650s
narrative_blocks.body: He served as Governor of Connecticut Colony in 1655 and 1658, and as Deputy Governor in 1654, 1656, 1657, and 1659. He had been Treasurer of the Colony four years and Secretary five — one of the most important and influential men in early Connecticut.

narrative_blocks.header: Arrived Hartford 1636 — same year as Hooker
narrative_blocks.body: He emigrated from Burmington, Worcester, England in 1635, first holding a house in Cambridge, Massachusetts. He removed to Hartford in 1636 — the same year Thomas Hooker led his congregation there — and was immediately made a magistrate of the colony.

narrative_blocks.header: Hartford land grant: seven acres of meadow
narrative_blocks.body: In the February 1639/40 land inventory of Hartford he held seven acres and two roods on which his dwelling house stood, plus a 90-acre parcel, twelve acres at Hockanum, and three acres in the swamp by the Great River. He moved to Wethersfield in 1646.

cross_connections.display_label: His Welles-line descendant married this Hooker great-granddaughter
an ancestor of the geologist Raphael Pumpelly
